diagnosis.root_cause: banco.sqlite corrupted, most likely by the unexpected shutdown
reasoning: SQLITE_CORRUPT: 'database disk image is malformed' in logs
PRAGMA integrity_check FAILED (index inconsistency at row 4213)
an unexpected-shutdown/dirty-boot event fired ~30s before the first error
disk at 61% — not a disk-full cause, points to the dirty shutdown
app uptime only 120s — it restarted into a broken database

alternative_hypotheses.why_less_likely: disk_percent is 61% — plenty of free space

impact.business_impact: Sistema indisponivel; risco de perder ate 24h de dados desde o ultimo backup

actions.action: Restore banco.sqlite from the last backup (24h old)
actions.rollback: Keep the corrupted file aside; restore from an older backup if needed
actions.estimated_impact: Restores service; loses data written since the last backup
actions.risks: Up to 24h of data loss (entries since last backup)
actions.implementation: Copy banco.sqlite -> banco.corrupt.sqlite, then copy the newest file in backups/ to banco.sqlite and restart the server

actions.action: Verify the restored file and tighten backup cadence
actions.estimated_impact: Confirms recovery and prevents a repeat of the 24h gap
actions.implementation: PRAGMA integrity_check on the restored DB; schedule hourly backups via Task Scheduler
